channel.title: Drupal Core
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal Core.

item.title: #3101714: Link field display should show plaintext URLs longer than 80 chars
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +67/−8 · php +67/−8
  • Diff: 0c75f8e
  • Discussion: #3101714 · 24 contributors · 74 comments
  • Followers: 31

Summary

Link fields configured to display URLs as plain text (the "URL only" or "Show URL as plain text" formatter options) no longer truncate the URL at 80 characters. Previously, the default 80-character trim was applied regardless of display mode, which silently produced broken URLs whenever a URL exceeded that length and had no separate title. The trim remains active for standard hyperlinked display, where truncating the visible text does not break the underlying link. This change affects both new and existing link fields at render time.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: Existing link fields with url_only or url_plain enabled and a non-zero trim_length will now render full URLs at display time, regardless of the stored trim_length value.

Technical details

In LinkFormatter::viewElements(), the call to Unicode::truncate() was previously guarded only by !empty($settings['trim_length']). The condition is now !empty($settings['trim_length']) && empty($settings['url_plain']) && empty($settings['url_only']), so trimming fires only when the link is rendered as a standard anchor tag. The same guard was added to settingsSummary(), so the "Link text trimmed to N characters" message is also suppressed when it would be inaccurate. In the formatter settings form, the trim_length element gains a #states rule that hides it in the UI when url_only is checked. The default value of trim_length (80) is unchanged in defaultSettings(); what changed is whether that value is honored at render time. Tests in LinkFormatterDisplayTest and LinkSeparateFormatterDisplayTest add two new input fixtures: a URL longer than 80 characters with an explicit title, and the same URL with no title. Expected results confirm that plain-text and URL-only display modes now render the full URL while anchor-tag display still applies the trim.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: December 18, 2019
  • Committed: July 21, 2026 (6 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • pameeela
  • tobiasb (Gesellschaft zur Entwicklung von Dingen)
  • kapilv (OpenSense Labs)
  • shobhit_juyal (Material)
  • vlyalko
  • angrytoast (Tableau)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • lmoeni (IT.NRW)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • mherchel (Agileana)
  • prashant.c (QED42)
  • keshav patel (OpenSense Labs)
  • arun.k (gai Technologies Pvt Ltd)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

The thread had been open for over seven years and went through several design pivots. The original report flagged that plain-text URLs were being broken, but the issue stalled partly because the bug was hard to reproduce on a fresh install without a specific template. The key design shift came from alexpott (comment #15621679), who argued that the formatter does two fundamentally different things: rendering an anchor tag (where trimming the visible text is harmless) versus emitting raw text (where trimming produces a broken URL). That framing redirected the fix from simply removing the 80-character default entirely to a targeted bypass that only skips the trim in plain-text display modes, preserving the trim for anchor-tag rendering where it still makes sense.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3284817: Adopt vincentlanglet/twig-cs-fixer for Twig coding standards
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 184 files, +527/−308 · twig +300/−303 · lock +78/−1 · yml +50/−1 · sh +46 · php +39 · json +8/−3 · gitignore +3 · txt +3
  • Diff: f9bfac3
  • Discussion: #3284817 · 21 contributors · 82 comments
  • Followers: 35

Summary

Drupal core has adopted vincentlanglet/twig-cs-fixer as a require-dev dependency to enforce Twig coding standards, closing the last gap in core's automated linting suite alongside PHP_CodeSniffer, ESLint, and Stylelint. As part of the change, approximately 176 existing core Twig files were reformatted to comply with the new rules. Developers contributing Twig templates to core (or wanting to follow core conventions in contrib or custom themes) now have an official tool and a reference configuration to work from. Site owners are not affected; the reformatted templates produce identical rendered output.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New Composer scripts composer twigcs and composer twigcs-fix for checking and auto-fixing Twig coding standard violations.

Technical details

The new core/.twig-cs-fixer.php configuration file registers Drupal's TwigTransTokenParser so the linter understands the {% trans %} tag, excludes tests/ directories from linting, and applies the TwigCsFixer standard (which implements the official Twig coding standards at twig.symfony.com). Two rules are overridden for Drupal: IndentRule(spaceRatio: 2) for 2-space indentation and CompactHashRule(TRUE) to require no spaces around inline hash literals (e.g. {key: value} rather than { 'key': value }). allowNonFixableRules() is called so the config works in both lint and fix modes.

Two Composer scripts are added: composer twigcs runs a lint check and composer twigcs-fix auto-fixes violations. A new 🧹 Twig Coding standards (twig-cs-fixer) job is added to the GitLab CI Lint stage; on merge-request pipelines it only triggers when .twig files, core/.twig-cs-fixer.php, or composer.json/composer.lock change. Cache artifacts use the file core/.twigcsfixercache, which is added to .gitignore and wired into the existing CI cache download/upload pattern alongside the PHP and JS caches. The commit-code-check.sh pre-commit script is also updated to run the linter against any staged .twig files, and to re-lint all files when the config itself changes.

The bulk reformatting of the ~176 existing core files normalized four categories of style: {% include 'tmpl' %} tags became {{ include('tmpl') }} function calls; {% include 'tmpl' with {...} only %} became {{ include('tmpl', {...}, with_context: false) }}; double-quoted string literals became single-quoted; and hash array keys were converted from quoted form ({'key': value}) to unquoted form ({key: value}) where the key is a valid identifier. Trailing commas were also added to multi-line array and argument lists.

Upgrade

No changes are required for existing sites. Developers contributing Twig templates to Drupal core can check their files with:

composer twigcs

And auto-fix violations with:

composer twigcs-fix

The configuration lives in core/.twig-cs-fixer.php. Custom modules or themes that want to adopt the same standards can reference that file as a starting point, though the TwigTransTokenParser registration it contains is only needed when linting templates that use Drupal's {% trans %} tag.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 9, 2022
  • Committed: July 21, 2026 (4 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • saidatom (Squarebit)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • wotnak (Smartbees)
  • rodrigoaguilera
  • xjm (HeroDevs)
  • feuerwagen (Keen On Digital)
  • papagrande (VTP Ventures)
  • thejimbirch (Kanopi Studios)
  • nod_ (Très Bien Tech)
  • alex.skrypnyk (DrevOps)
  • lisa.rae (Vardot)
  • grimreaper (Smile)
  • joelpittet (The University of British Columbia)
  • johnalbin
  • murz (EPAM)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • pdureau (Beyris)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

The issue was open for nearly three years and required sustained effort from several contributors. feuerwagen noticed the upstream vincentlanglet/twig-cs-fixer project lacked a published security policy (a blocker for adding any new Drupal core dependency) and filed a GitHub issue to request one; the maintainer adopted it within days, allowing the evaluation to proceed. grimreaper carried the MR through multiple rebases across major version changes, ultimately creating fresh MRs each time the old branches became impossible to rebase, and updated the dependency to version 4.0.0 for the final round. johnalbin joined late and reviewed every non-Twig file in the diff, resolved the with_context: false naming question, added the CI optimization that limits the linting job to pipelines where Twig files actually changed, and pushed the last round of corrections before RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3485030: Avoid saving menu links through node form when they do not change
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +43/−2 · php +43/−2
  • Diff: c4d7966
  • Discussion: #3485030 · 11 contributors · 48 comments
  • Followers: 22

Summary

When an editor saves a node through the node edit form without making any changes to the associated menu link, Drupal previously saved the menu_link_content entity unconditionally, triggering postSave() and invalidating the config:system.menu.MENUNAME cache tag every time. Because menus are rendered on most pages, this caused broad, unnecessary cache invalidation across CDNs and page caches. After this fix, the menu link entity is only saved when something actually changed, preventing spurious invalidation of menu cache tags on unrelated node saves.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The fix is in MenuUiHelper::menuUiNodeSave() in menu_ui. Previously, $entity->save() was called unconditionally at the end of the function regardless of whether any menu link fields had changed. The fix splits that single save call into two paths. For the branch that handles updating an existing menu_link_content entity (when $values['entity_id'] is set), $entity->setOriginal(clone $entity) is called immediately after loading, which arms the entity's change tracking. For the else branch (pending-revision handling), a $default_revision_change flag is computed by comparing $entity->isDefaultRevision() with $node->isDefaultRevision(), and $entity->save() is now gated on $default_revision_change || $entity->hasTranslationChanges(). Using hasTranslationChanges() alone was not sufficient because it does not detect a default-to-non-default revision transition; that required the explicit separate flag. An earlier approach proposed implementing the skip logic inside MenuTreeStorage::doSave(), but that was rejected because the storage layer is not declared internal and because MenuLinkContent::postSave() writes only the active-language title into the tree storage, making a purely storage-level comparison unreliable for multilingual sites (see comment #26). The new test coverage in MenuNodeFormWidgetTest verifies the cache_tags.invalidator.checksum service before and after various scenarios: no-op saves do not increment the checksum; adding, modifying weight, and deleting a menu link do. The fix also uncovered a separate bug in how content moderation and non-default revisions interacted, which was resolved as a prerequisite in #3499181.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: November 1, 2024
  • Committed: July 20, 2026 (1 year and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • achap
  • kristiaanvandeneynde (Factorial.io)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • andy_w (Numiko)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • dcam
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

berdir drove the final implementation after the original approach (changing MenuTreeStorage::doSave() return value, proposed by achap) was found to be a risky behavior change for multilingual sites. In comment #26, berdir explained that MenuLinkContent::postSave() only writes the active-language title into the tree storage, so any storage-level equality check would silently drop multilingual data. berdir opened a new MR targeting menu_ui instead, then hit a test failure tracing back to a content-moderation bug introduced years earlier (credited partly to berdir's own past work) that allowed saving a default revision as a non-default revision. That was spun out and fixed as a prerequisite in #3499181. After that blocker landed, berdir switched the change-detection logic from explicit field comparisons to hasTranslationChanges(), then discovered that method misses the default-revision flag, requiring the explicit $default_revision_change guard. achap closed the original MR once the community agreed the storage-layer approach was not safe.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3611653: Upsert fails after upgrade from 11.3 to 11.4
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Critical bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +153/−12 · php +153/−12
  • Diff: 220ee9f
  • Discussion: #3611653 · 4 contributors · 42 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Upgrading from Drupal 11.3 to 11.4 broke database upsert operations where every field passed to the upsert is also part of the key (the "degenerate" case, meaning there are no non-key columns to update on conflict). MySQL returned a syntax error because the generated SQL ended with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE and nothing after it. The fix restores this as a supported pattern across all database drivers: the statement now silently ignores duplicates rather than crashing.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause was an accidental behavioral change introduced by #2547493, which added composite key support to Upsert queries in 11.4. That commit used array_combine() to rekey $insert_fields from a zero-based numeric array to a string-keyed associative array. Once the array had string keys, the existing unset($insert_fields[$key]) loop successfully removed the key columns from the update list. When all fields are key fields, this left $update empty, and the MySQL driver emitted INSERT INTO ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with no assignments, which is a syntax error. In 11.3 the unset call was effectively a no-op because the array was still zero-indexed, so the key column remained in the update list and MySQL accepted the statement (performing a no-op update on conflict).

The fix introduces an explicit check for the empty-update case in each driver. In core/modules/mysql/src/Driver/Database/mysql/Upsert.php, when $update_fields is empty after removing key columns, the query is built as INSERT IGNORE INTO ... instead of INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE. In the PostgreSQL drivers (core/modules/pgsql and core/modules/sqlite), the same empty check switches from ON CONFLICT (...) DO UPDATE SET ... to ON CONFLICT (...) DO NOTHING. Three new kernel tests cover the degenerate case: testDegeneratedSingleValueUpsert, testDegeneratedMultiValueUpsert, and testDegeneratedCompositeKeyUpsert.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 17, 2026
  • Committed: July 20, 2026 (2 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • john.oltman
  • cilefen
  • mondrake
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

john.oltman, who reported the regression, did key investigative work in the thread: they pinpointed the exact commit that introduced the breakage (#2547493), confirmed through a debugger that the behavior difference was caused by the numeric-to-string rekey via array_combine(), and tested each proposed fix end-to-end. mondrake picked up the diagnosis, built the fix, and after an initial MR that still threw an exception for the degenerate case, revised the approach to use INSERT IGNORE / DO NOTHING instead of erroring out, then retracted an earlier proposal to deprecate the pattern once john.oltman demonstrated it is a legitimate multi-row insert idiom with native DB support on all drivers.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3611853: Correct error deprecation message for \Drupal\Core\Htmx\HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxTrigger
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +4/−4 · php +4/−4
  • Diff: 4163754
  • Discussion: #3611853 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The deprecation notice for HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxTrigger() incorrectly stated "There is no replacement" when the method was deprecated in Drupal 11.5.0. The correct replacement is HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxSource(). This fix updates both the PHPDoc @deprecated tag and the runtime @trigger_error() message to name that replacement, so developers who encounter the warning know exactly what to use instead.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The getHtmxTrigger() method in \Drupal\Core\Htmx\HtmxRequestInfoTrait was deprecated as part of #3607711 (which deprecates several HTMX-related methods in 11.5.0). That original commit mistakenly wrote "There is no replacement" in both the PHPDoc @deprecated annotation and the @trigger_error() call inside the method body. The actual replacement is HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxSource().

The fix updates the PHPDoc tag to read Use HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxSource() instead. and updates the runtime error string to match. The unit test assertion in HtmxRequestInfoTraitTest::testGetHtmxTrigger() is updated to expect the corrected string. The change is in 11.x only, as the deprecations do not exist on main.

Upgrade

If your code calls HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxTrigger(), migrate to HtmxRequestInfoTrait::getHtmxSource() before Drupal 12.0.0.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 19, 2026
  • Committed: July 20, 2026

Key contributors

  • fathershawn (Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3611529: Moderation state field definitions can have an incorrect target bundle
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +80/−6 · php +80/−6
  • Diff: 28d2798
  • Discussion: #3611529 · 3 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Sites running Content Moderation with two or more moderated bundles could end up with incorrect moderation_state field definitions, where one bundle's definition reported the wrong target bundle. This matters because the target bundle is used to derive the associated workflow from a field definition. The fix ensures each bundle receives its own correctly scoped definition.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is a shared-reference mutation in ContentModerationHooks::entityBundleFieldInfo(). The hook received the shared $base_field_definitions array and called setTargetBundle($bundle) directly on the moderation_state entry before returning it. Because base field definitions are objects, this mutated the shared instance, so the last bundle to be processed would overwrite the target bundle on the same object that earlier bundles had already cached.

This latent bug was surfaced by #3537962, which introduced per-bundle caching in EntityFieldManager::getFieldDefinitions(). Under that caching model, builds for different bundles reuse the same base field definition objects, making the mutation observable across bundles.

The fix is a one-line change: replace the direct return with clone $base_field_definitions['moderation_state'], giving each bundle invocation its own copy. The explicit setTargetBundle($bundle) call was also removed because the entity field manager already applies the target bundle to every definition after collecting bundle field info, making the call in the hook redundant. A new kernel test ModerationStateFieldTest::testFieldDefinitionBundles() creates three moderated node types and asserts that each bundle's moderation_state field definition carries the correct target bundle ID.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 17, 2026
  • Committed: July 20, 2026 (3 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • clayfreeman (Library Market)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3595652: Deprecate module.inc contents
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 21 files, +158/−112 · php +155/−111 · yml +3/−1
  • Diff: daf0e8e
  • Discussion: #3595652 · 6 contributors · 27 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

The two procedural functions in module.inc that manage module weights, module_set_weight() and module_config_sort(), are deprecated in Drupal 11.5.0 and will be removed in Drupal 13.0.0. Their logic has been moved into a new Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight service, which provides equivalent set() and sort() methods. Modules that call either procedural function directly need to switch to the service before upgrading to Drupal 13.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: ModuleInstaller::__construct() gained a new nullable ?ModuleWeight $moduleWeight parameter; it will be required in drupal:12.0.0.
  • Deprecation: module_set_weight() and module_config_sort() in module.inc deprecated in drupal:11.5.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; replaced by Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight::set() and ::sort().
  • New API: New Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight service with set(string $module, int $weight): void and sort(array $data): array methods.

Technical details

The new Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight class lives in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Extension/ModuleWeight.php and is registered as an autowired service. It accepts ModuleHandlerInterface and ConfigFactoryInterface via constructor injection. ModuleWeight::set(string $module, int $weight): void replicates the logic previously in module_set_weight(), including writing to core.extension config and updating the live module list in the module handler. ModuleWeight::sort(array $data): array replicates the sign-prefix plus zero-padded integer plus name string-sort algorithm from module_config_sort().

The procedural functions in module.inc now contain only a @trigger_error deprecation notice followed by a delegation call to \Drupal::service(ModuleWeight::class). All internal callers across Drupal core (including ModuleInstaller, ExcludedModulesEventSubscriber, the installer's install_finished(), content_translation.install, views.install, and several test classes) have been updated to use the service directly.

ModuleInstaller::__construct() gained a new nullable ?ModuleWeight $moduleWeight parameter at the end of its signature; a deprecation notice is triggered if the argument is absent when the class is constructed, and the argument will be required in Drupal 12.0.0. ExcludedModulesEventSubscriber similarly gained ModuleWeight as a new constructor argument and its service definition in core.services.yml was updated accordingly.

Upgrade

Replace calls to module_set_weight() with the ModuleWeight service:

// Before (deprecated since 11.5.0):
module_set_weight('mymodule', 10);

// After:
use Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight;
\Drupal::service(ModuleWeight::class)->set('mymodule', 10);

Replace calls to module_config_sort() with the ModuleWeight service:

// Before (deprecated since 11.5.0):
$sorted = module_config_sort($modules);

// After:
use Drupal\Core\Extension\ModuleWeight;
$sorted = \Drupal::service(ModuleWeight::class)->sort($modules);

If you extend or directly instantiate ModuleInstaller, add ModuleWeight as the final constructor argument. See the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3595653.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 11, 2026
  • First commit: July 14, 2026 (1 month later)
  • Last commit: July 20, 2026 (5 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588274: Remove Tags vocabulary from Standard profile and recipe
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +0/−16 · yml +0/−15 · php +0/−1
  • Diff: 0654319
  • Discussion: #3588274 · 6 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

New Drupal installations using the Standard profile or the standard recipe no longer include a "Tags" taxonomy vocabulary. The content_editor role also no longer receives the create terms in tags and edit terms in tags permissions. Sites that need a Tags vocabulary must now create one manually after installation. This is part of a broader effort to pare down the Standard profile to a minimal baseline, following the earlier removal of the Article and Basic page content types (#3587118) and the Comment module dependency (#3588276).

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Config change: The taxonomy.vocabulary.tags config object and the create terms in tags / edit terms in tags permissions on content_editor are no longer included in the Standard profile or standard recipe.

Technical details

The change deletes taxonomy.vocabulary.tags.yml from the standard recipe config directory. The content_editor role config (content_editor.yml) is updated to drop its dependency on both taxonomy.vocabulary.tags config and the taxonomy module itself, and to remove the create terms in tags and edit terms in tags permissions. The same permission removals are mirrored in the standard recipe's own role config snapshot. The StandardTest functional test is updated to remove taxonomy.vocabulary.tags from the expected list of installed config objects. The change was motivated by the fact that after the removal of the Article content type (which previously used the Tags field), the vocabulary was left floating with nothing referencing it.

Upgrade

If your site's deployment scripts, migration code, or tests assume the tags vocabulary exists in a fresh Standard install, you must either create the vocabulary explicitly or add it via a custom recipe/install hook. The content_editor role will also no longer have taxonomy term permissions, so any code granting that role and expecting tag editing rights must be updated.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 4, 2026
  • First commit: May 26, 2026 (21 days later)
  • Last commit: July 19, 2026 (1 month and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • gábor hojtsy (Acquia)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • damienmckenna
  • larowlan (PreviousNext)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3607711: Deprecate \Drupal\Core\Htmx\Htmx methods
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +81/−10 · php +81/−10
  • Diff: 33be965
  • Discussion: #3607711 · 7 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

Drupal 11.5 deprecates a set of methods on the \Drupal\Core\Htmx\Htmx builder class and the HtmxRequestInfoTrait trait. These methods correspond to features that htmx v4 removes or restructures, and they will be removed entirely in Drupal 12.0. Modules or custom code that call any of the deprecated methods will receive PHP deprecation notices on Drupal 11.5 and will break on Drupal 12.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Eleven methods across \Drupal\Core\Htmx\Htmx and HtmxRequestInfoTrait deprecated in drupal:11.5.0 and removed from drupal:12.0.0; triggerAfterSettleHeader() and triggerAfterSwapHeader() replaced by triggerHeader(), the rest have no PHP-level replacement.

Technical details

The commit adds @trigger_error() calls with E_USER_DEPRECATED and corresponding @deprecated PHPDoc blocks to eleven methods across two classes in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Htmx/.

In \Drupal\Core\Htmx\Htmx: triggerAfterSettleHeader() and triggerAfterSwapHeader() are deprecated in favour of triggerHeader(). The methods disabledElt(), disinherit(), ext(), history(), inherit(), params(), prompt(), and request() are deprecated with no direct PHP replacement (the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3583674 documents the htmx-v4-compatible approaches). request() is notable because the underlying hx-request attribute was removed outright in htmx v4.

In \Drupal\Core\Htmx\HtmxRequestInfoTrait: getHtmxPrompt() and getHtmxTrigger() are deprecated with no replacement, because htmx v4 no longer sends the corresponding HX-Prompt and HX-Trigger headers in the same way.

All deprecations target drupal:11.5.0 and removal at drupal:12.0.0. Existing unit tests in HtmxTest, HtmxHeaderTest, and HtmxRequestInfoTest were updated to use #[IgnoreDeprecations] and expectUserDeprecationMessage() so they continue to exercise the deprecated code paths without failing. This work is a prerequisite for #3555916, which will upgrade Drupal core to htmx v4 on the main (Drupal 12) branch and remove these methods.

Upgrade

Replace deprecated Htmx method calls as follows:

// Before (deprecated in 11.5, removed in 12.0):
$htmx->triggerAfterSettleHeader('myEvent');
$htmx->triggerAfterSwapHeader('myEvent');

// After:
$htmx->triggerHeader('myEvent');

For disabledElt(), disinherit(), ext(), history(), inherit(), params(), prompt(), and request(), no equivalent method exists in the PHP API. Follow the htmx v4 migration guidance in the change record at https://www.drupal.org/node/3583674.

For getHtmxPrompt() and getHtmxTrigger() (from HtmxRequestInfoTrait): there is no replacement. Remove calls to these methods.

To write code that works on both Drupal 11 and 12, you can use DeprecationHelper:

use Drupal\Component\Utility\DeprecationHelper;

DeprecationHelper::backwardsCompatibleCall(
  currentVersion: \Drupal::VERSION,
  deprecatedVersion: '11.5',
  currentCallable: fn() => /* new approach */,
  deprecatedCallable: fn() => $htmx->triggerAfterSettleHeader('myEvent'),
);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 1, 2026
  • Committed: July 17, 2026 (15 days later)

Key contributors

  • fathershawn (Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center)
  • sourav_paul (Innoraft)
  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • macsim
  • godotislate
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

Collaboration

fathershawn opened the issue and drove the work. quietone clarified the correct deprecation workflow (deprecations land in 11.x and main separately; the htmx v4 upgrade itself is a separate commit), which led fathershawn to restructure the approach. macsim caught that Drupal 11.4 had already been released and the version strings needed to be bumped from 11.4.0 to 11.5.0, and also identified that the test failures were uncaught deprecation notices rather than genuine test failures. fathershawn then added #[IgnoreDeprecations] attributes to the relevant test methods, following core convention.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586832: Allow kernel test classes to define their own routes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +74/−7 · php +74/−7
  • Diff: f44b16f
  • Discussion: #3586832 · 7 contributors · 25 comments
  • Followers: 11

Summary

Kernel test classes can now define their own HTTP routes by placing Symfony's #[Route] PHP attribute on methods in the test class. Previously, a test author needing a custom route for a kernel test had to create a separate test module just to host that one route. With this change, the test class itself acts as the controller, and test properties are directly accessible in the route method without any database round-trips or mocking.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: AttributeRouteDiscovery::createControllerRouteCollection() and getReflectionClass() widened from private to protected, enabling subclassing.
  • New API: Kernel test classes can now place Symfony #[Route] attributes on methods to register routes in the test container without a separate test module.

Technical details

In KernelTestBase::register(), a new KernelTestAttributeRouteDiscovery service is now registered in the test container, tagged as event_subscriber. This @internal class extends AttributeRouteDiscovery and overrides collectRoutes() to scan only the specific kernel test class, which is passed as a constructor string argument. The service is registered with static::class so each test class has its own discovery instance.

To enable the subclass, two methods in AttributeRouteDiscovery were widened from private to protected: createControllerRouteCollection(\ReflectionClass $class) and getReflectionClass(string $className). These are the only diff-visible changes to AttributeRouteDiscovery itself; AttributeRouteDiscovery is not marked @internal, making this a public API surface change.

The KernelTestHttpRequestTest demonstrates the feature: a $testMarkup property set in the test method is read by exampleRoute(), which is decorated with #[Route(path: '/kernel-test-example', ...)]. This proves that test instance state is shared between the test body and the route handler because both run in the same process. Routes meant to be shared across multiple kernel tests can be placed in a trait and used via use.

Upgrade

If you subclass AttributeRouteDiscovery and relied on createControllerRouteCollection() or getReflectionClass() being private (e.g. via reflection to access them), they are now protected and directly available to subclasses.

To define a route in a kernel test class:

use Symfony\Component\Routing\Attribute\Route;

class MyKernelTest extends KernelTestBase {

  protected string $myValue = '';

  public function testSomething(): void {
    $this->myValue = 'hello';
    $this->drupalGet('my-test-route');
    $this->assertSession()->pageTextContains('hello');
  }

  #[Route(
    path: '/my-test-route',
    name: 'my_test.example',
    requirements: ['_access' => 'TRUE'],
  )]
  public function myController(): array {
    return ['#markup' => $this->myValue];
  }

}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 25, 2026
  • Committed: July 17, 2026 (2 months and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • dcam

Collaboration

smustgrave caught a real bug during local testing before the change landed: KernelTestAttributeRouteDiscovery was calling createControllerRouteCollection(), which was still private in the parent class, producing a fatal error. godotislate responded by widening both private methods to protected and refactoring the constructor to accept a class name string rather than an object. dcam verified the feature by converting a real existing route (binary_file_response_test.download) to an in-test route and confirming the test still passed. joachim raised the question of how to share a route across multiple kernel tests; godotislate suggested placing the #[Route]-annotated method in a PHP trait.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3547724: Throw an exception when unsupported procedural functions are defined in views
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +17/−107 · php +17/−107
  • Diff: a06956c
  • Discussion: #3547724 · 5 contributors · 17 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

Support for the access callback key in Views handler plugin definitions has been removed in Drupal 12.0.0. Any custom Views handler that still declares an access callback in its definition array will now throw an AccessException when access() is called, rather than silently invoking the procedural function. Developers must replace procedural access callbacks with a custom access() method on their handler class.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: HandlerBase::access() now throws AccessException (rather than invoking the callback or returning TRUE) when access callback is present in the handler definition; the key is no longer supported.

Technical details

In HandlerBase::access() (located in core/modules/views/src/Plugin/views/HandlerBase.php), the old code checked function_exists($this->definition['access callback']), triggered a @trigger_error deprecation, and then called the procedural function. The new code replaces all of that with a single unconditional throw new AccessException(...) whenever the access callback key is present in the definition at all, regardless of whether the function exists. The check for function_exists is gone, meaning there is no longer any execution path that runs the callback.

The two test helper functions views_test_data_handler_test_access_callback() and views_test_data_handler_test_access_callback_argument() have been deleted from core/modules/views/tests/modules/views_test_data/views_test_data.module, and the corresponding functional testAccess() test in HandlerTest has been removed. A new unit test testAccessThrowsExceptionWithAccessCallback() in HandlerTest (unit variant) covers the exception path. The original deprecation was introduced in drupal:11.3.0 referencing #3539918.

Upgrade

Any custom Views handler definition that sets access callback must be updated. Instead of:

// Old: in hook_views_data() or a derived views data class
$data['my_table']['my_field']['field']['access callback'] = 'my_module_access_callback';

Remove the access callback key entirely and override access() on the handler class:

// New: in the handler plugin class
public function access(AccountInterface $account) {
  // Your access logic here.
  return $account->hasPermission('my permission');
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 20, 2025
  • Committed: July 17, 2026 (10 months later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • lendude (iO)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

Collaboration

During review, nicxvan noted that the function_exists() guard in the original deprecation code should also be dropped to prevent accidentally granting access if the callback no longer existed. berdir and lendude discussed whether to keep the functional test coverage: berdir explained that removing the generic mechanism meant every handler type would need its own custom test plugin to exercise access logic, making coverage retention high-effort. lendude agreed that removing the tests made sense given that trade-off, and catch re-titled the issue to reflect that the final approach throws an exception rather than silently defaulting to false.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3029122: Drupal returns a 500 Internal error when 'destination' url query value is numeric and less than 6 length
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +6/−1 · php +6/−1
  • Diff: ecaa384
  • Discussion: #3029122 · 8 contributors · 43 comments
  • Followers: 15

Summary

Drupal threw a 500 Internal Server Error when a form redirect used a ?destination= query value that was a short numeric string (for example ?destination=18). This happened when visiting URLs like /user/login?destination=18 and then successfully logging in. The bug is now fixed: numeric destination values are handled correctly and Drupal redirects as expected after form submission.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The root cause is PHP's parse_url() interpreting short numeric URI paths differently depending on digit length. parse_url('base:12345', PHP_URL_SCHEME) returns 'base' (correct), but parse_url('base:1234', PHP_URL_SCHEME) returns null because PHP parses it as a host name plus port rather than a scheme plus path. As a result, the scheme check in UnroutedUrlAssembler::assemble() silently failed for short numeric paths, and Drupal fell through to invalid-URI error handling with a fatal 500.

The fix replaces the parse_url($uri, PHP_URL_SCHEME) === 'base' check with strncasecmp($uri, 'base:', 5) === 0 && parse_url($uri, PHP_URL_SCHEME) !== FALSE. The strncasecmp call reliably detects the base: scheme prefix regardless of what follows it, while the secondary parse_url !== FALSE guard still rejects entirely malformed URIs that PHP cannot parse at all. Two test cases covering base:1978 were added to providerTestAssembleWithLocalUri().

The fix was backported to 11.4.x and 11.x in addition to the main (12.x) branch. A follow-up issue (#3591074) tracks replacing broader usages of parse_url across Drupal core with the PHP 8.5 URI extension.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: January 29, 2019
  • Committed: July 17, 2026 (7 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • thihathit
  • chi
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • steven jones (ComputerMinds)
  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

steven jones initially proposed using the new PHP 8.5 URI extension (ext-uri) to replace parse_url entirely. After alexpott reviewed the merge request and provided feedback, the approach was changed to a simpler string prefix comparison. That narrower approach had the important side-effect of making the fix backportable to Drupal 11, which a PHP-8.5-only solution would not have been. steven jones noted the pivot explicitly: "I've tweaked the approach based on excellent feedback from @alexpott, and so this actually becomes possible to backport this fix too."

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3611084: DrupalApplication should use the compiled container
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +31/−28 · php +31/−28
  • Diff: 854ccc0
  • Discussion: #3611084 · 3 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

CLI commands run through vendor/bin/dr (and tools such as Drush that bootstrap Drupal the same way) now use the compiled, cached container instead of rebuilding it from scratch on every invocation. Previously DrupalApplication::bootstrap() always passed allow_dumping=FALSE to DrupalKernel, forcing a full ContainerBuilder build even in production. The fix makes normal CLI use fast while keeping the uncompiled-container behaviour available for kernel tests that need it.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New DrupalApplicationTesterTrait in Drupal\KernelTests for kernel test classes that need to invoke vendor/bin/dr commands, replacing ad-hoc inline helpers.

Technical details

DrupalApplication::bootstrap() previously hardcoded new DrupalKernel('prod', $this->classloader, FALSE), disabling container dumping unconditionally. The original motivation was that KernelTestBase::register() mutates the container, so loading a cached copy during kernel tests would reuse the wrong container. The fix replaces the hardcoded values with context lookups: new DrupalKernel($this->context['kernel.environment'] ?? 'prod', $this->classloader, $this->context['kernel.allow_dumping'] ?? TRUE). Production CLI invocations get the compiled container by default; test harnesses opt out by passing kernel.environment => 'testing' and kernel.allow_dumping => FALSE in the context array.

A new DrupalApplicationTesterTrait (in Drupal\KernelTests) centralises this opt-out for kernel test classes. The trait provides a private applicationTester(array $context = []) method that includes vendor/bin/dr, injects the two testing context keys, and returns a Symfony ApplicationTester. The duplicate inline applicationTester() helpers that existed in StatusCommandTest and ConsoleTest were removed in favour of the shared trait.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 15, 2026
  • Committed: July 17, 2026 (1 day and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • moshe weitzman

Collaboration

catch noted in a comment that Drush was already hitting this slow path, having profiled it the previous week, and that the fix should make CLI operations noticeably snappier across the board. moshe weitzman independently arrived at the same resolution while working on a separate module/theme install command issue (#3594334), confirming the approach.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2863785: Avoid PHP notice when comparing config without uuid
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +40/−1 · php +40/−1
  • Diff: c8bda45
  • Discussion: #2863785 · 16 contributors · 48 comments
  • Followers: 27

Summary

During config synchronization, Drupal's StorageComparer was throwing a PHP "Undefined index: uuid" notice whenever a config entity in the active (target) store was missing its uuid key. This could happen with old configs migrated from early Drupal 8, contrib module settings files that omit a UUID, or manually created database entries. The fix suppresses the notice and, as a side effect, treats the missing UUID as a mismatch, causing the config system to recreate the item with a proper UUID on the next import.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The problem was in StorageComparer::addChangelistUpdate() in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Config/StorageComparer.php. The UUID comparison read $target_data['uuid'] directly, which triggers a PHP notice when the key is absent. The fix replaces that bare access with a null-coalescing expression: ($target_data['uuid'] ?? NULL). Because no real UUID string equals null, a missing UUID in the target store is treated as a UUID mismatch. The existing branch for UUID mismatches schedules a recreate (a paired delete and create operation), which self-heals the target store by replacing the UUID-less entry with a properly-generated one on import. A new unit test testChangelistUuidMissMatch() in StorageComparerTest covers both the missing-UUID case (triggering recreate) and the changed-UUID case (also triggering recreate). Note: alexpott reviewed a draft version that mutated $target_data['uuid'] in-place and objected to modifying the target data; neclimdul reworked the approach to the null-coalesce-only form that landed.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 24, 2017
  • Committed: July 16, 2026 (9 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • neclimdul (APQC)
  • john cook (CTI Digital)
  • claudiu.cristea (Webikon)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)

Collaboration

The issue spent years in a stalled state because, as alexpott noted in 2018, there was no reliable way to reproduce the condition without direct database manipulation. Multiple contributors (including claudiu.cristea, achap, and jungle) added context about how configs without UUIDs ended up in production: old Drupal 8 migrations, contrib modules like admin_toolbar and captcha exporting settings configs without UUID keys, and config_split-based workflows. neclimdul eventually posted an updated patch for Drupal 10 with a unit test and opened a merge request in 2025. During review, alexpott objected to one version that wrote NULL back into $target_data['uuid'], arguing it incorrectly mutated the target data; neclimdul addressed this by switching to the null-coalesce approach. john.oltman then corrected the issue title to reflect that the MR suppresses the notice rather than actively repairing storage, a distinction alexpott acknowledged.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3532506: Never bypass basic validation when saving a config object
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +29/−12 · php +29/−12
  • Diff: e798c54
  • Discussion: #3532506 · 8 contributors · 28 comments
  • Followers: 10

Summary

Passing $has_trusted_data = TRUE to Config::save() was intended to skip schema-based type coercion (the expensive castValue() pass), but it also accidentally skipped the basic sanity check that prevents non-scalar types such as objects and streams from being serialized into the config storage. As a result, calling $config->set('foo', new stdClass())->save(TRUE) would silently write PHP-serialized objects to the database. This is now fixed: basic type validation always runs regardless of the $has_trusted_data flag, so any unsupported type throws UnsupportedDataTypeConfigException at save time. Note that the $has_trusted_data parameter itself was deprecated in Drupal 11.4 by #3347842, so this fix also guards the remaining lifecycle of that deprecated path.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug was in the conditional inside Config::save() in core/lib/Drupal/Core/Config/Config.php. The original structure nested both castValue() and validateValue() inside a single if (!$has_trusted_data) guard, meaning the else branch that calls validateValue() was unreachable when trusted data was passed. The fix moves the condition so that only the schema-based castValue() call is guarded by !$has_trusted_data: the condition becomes if (!$has_trusted_data && $this->typedConfigManager->hasConfigSchema($this->name)), with an unconditional else branch that always runs validateValue() on every top-level key. This ensures the invariant that config storage only ever contains nested arrays of scalar values, even when the caller opts out of schema enforcement. New test assertions in ConfigCRUDTest::testTrustedDataDeprecation() cover both the case where a config schema exists and the case where none is registered, confirming that UnsupportedDataTypeConfigException is thrown in both situations.

Upgrade

If you have code that calls $config->save(TRUE) and sets non-scalar values (objects, resources, etc.), that code will now throw UnsupportedDataTypeConfigException. Ensure all values are nested arrays of scalars before saving. Additionally, the $has_trusted_data parameter is deprecated as of Drupal 11.4 and will be removed in 13.0; migrate to calling $config->save() without arguments.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 26, 2025
  • Committed: July 14, 2026 (1 year and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • benjifisher (Harvard Web Publishing)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • dhruv.mittal (Innoraft)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • santanu mondal (Innoraft)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3603333: Errors when stream wrappers instantiate services in constructors because register() is called before container is built
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +104/−6 · php +92/−6 · yml +12
  • Diff: 3fb729a
  • Discussion: #3603333 · 5 contributors · 21 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

Installing a module that provides a stream wrapper calling \Drupal::service() in its constructor would throw a ServiceNotFoundException or ServiceCircularReferenceException fatal error in Drupal 11.4.x. Affected contrib modules include s3fs, gcsfs, media_avportal, rokka, and flysystem. The fix ensures stream wrapper services are never instantiated during container rebuilds, so all services are safely accessible when stream wrappers are eventually constructed.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The regression was introduced by #3571094 (Remove RegisterStreamWrappersPass). After that change, StreamWrapperManager::getClass() retrieved the class name by calling get_class($this->container->get($scheme)), which instantiated the stream wrapper service immediately. StreamWrapperManager::register() calls getClass() internally, and register() is invoked from DrupalKernel::initializeContainer() during a module install — before the new container has replaced the global container. Any stream wrapper whose __construct() called \Drupal::service() therefore asked the old container for services that only exist in the new one.

The fix reintroduces a dedicated compiler pass, StreamWrapperClassesPass, that collects stream wrapper class names from service definitions at container compile time (reading ContainerDefinition::getClass(), not instantiating anything) and injects the resulting $wrapperClasses array as a constructor argument to StreamWrapperManager. getClass($scheme) now does a simple array lookup ($this->wrapperClasses[$scheme] ?? FALSE), and register() iterates $wrapperClasses directly. Stream wrapper services are no longer instantiated during the registration phase. A new test module (stream_wrapper_service_test) adds a kernel test covering a stream wrapper that calls a service in its constructor during module install, preventing regression.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 17, 2026
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (26 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • cmlara (DM13 Security LLC)
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

cmlara (the s3fs maintainer who filed the issue) documented multiple affected contrib modules and confirmed that failed installs could leave sites in an unrecoverable state, providing concrete pressure and test cases. longwave identified the precise root cause — #3571094's removal of RegisterStreamWrappersPass — and authored the MR that reintroduced class-name collection at compile time. godotislate caught a test failure in the initial MR where the test stream wrapper was fetching stream_wrapper_manager from the old container, prompting longwave to refine the test to use a distinct aliased service so the fix was properly validated.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3606411: Return the correct typehint when Drupal::classResolver() is called with a class string
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +12/−16 · php +12/−16
  • Diff: 0daf93c
  • Discussion: #3606411 · 6 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

PHPDoc annotations on \Drupal::classResolver() and ClassResolverInterface::getInstanceFromDefinition() now use PHPStan generics and conditional return types, so static analysis tools and IDEs can infer the concrete class type when a class-string literal is passed. Before this change, both methods returned an untyped object (or ClassResolverInterface) regardless of argument, forcing callers to add manual /** @var MyClass $x */ casts. After this change those casts are unnecessary. This mirrors the equivalent fix applied to \Drupal::service() in #3490713.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: \Drupal::classResolver() and ClassResolverInterface::getInstanceFromDefinition() now carry PHPStan generic/conditional return-type annotations; callers passing a class-string literal get the concrete class inferred instead of object.

Technical details

In Drupal::classResolver(), the @param is now typed class-string<T>|null $class with a @template T of object declaration, and the @return uses the conditional form ($class is class-string<T> ? T : \Drupal\Core\DependencyInjection\ClassResolverInterface). In ClassResolverInterface::getInstanceFromDefinition(), the same pattern is applied with class-string<T>|string $definition (no null, since that method does not accept null) and @return ($definition is class-string<T> ? T : object).

As a side-effect, four call sites in layout_builder's LayoutBuilderHooks that previously needed explicit @var cast annotations (for InlineBlockEntityOperations and LayoutOverrideFieldHelper) were simplified to direct method chaining, because PHPStan now resolves the type automatically from the class-string literal passed to classResolver().

Upgrade

No runtime behavior changed. If your code uses \Drupal::classResolver(MyClass::class) or ClassResolverInterface::getInstanceFromDefinition(MyClass::class) and carries an inline @var cast to suppress PHPStan errors, that cast can now be removed:

// Before
/** @var \My\Module\MyClass $instance */
$instance = \Drupal::classResolver(\My\Module\MyClass::class);
$instance->doSomething();

// After (PHPStan infers \My\Module\MyClass automatically)
\Drupal::classResolver(\My\Module\MyClass::class)->doSomething();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 26, 2026
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (17 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mstrelan
  • kieran.cott (Reading Room)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • godotislate

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3152267: Invalid use of array union operator
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +76/−1 · php +76/−1
  • Diff: 8976ec4
  • Discussion: #3152267 · 8 contributors · 31 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

A bug in the Views module caused the site and view token types to be silently dropped whenever additional token types were passed to getAvailableGlobalTokens(). For example, a view using date tokens alongside the expected site tokens would lose all site tokens, producing empty output instead of the intended replacements. The fix ensures that site and view are always included in the available token set regardless of what other types are requested.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The method PluginBase::getAvailableGlobalTokens() in core/modules/views/src/Plugin/views/PluginBase.php used PHP's array union operator ($types += ['site', 'view']) to guarantee that site and view token types are always present. Because $types is a numerically-indexed array, the union operator compares by index: index 0 is already occupied by whatever the caller passed, so 'site' (which would go to index 0) is never appended. The fix replaces the union with array_merge($types, ['site', 'view']), which appends unconditionally and lets array_intersect_key downstream select the correct tokens. The proposed array_unique wrapper was dropped during review because the token info array is multi-dimensional and array_unique() raises an "array to string conversion" error on such structures. A @DataProvider-driven unit test was added to PluginBaseTest covering combinations of zero, one, and multiple token types to prevent regression.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 16, 2020
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (6 years and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • s_bhandari (Material)
  • jayelless (Xequals)
  • quietone (Xequals)
  • lendude (iO)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

During review, quietone attempted to add array_unique() as suggested in the issue body, but discovered that the token info array is multi-dimensional, causing PHP to throw an "array to string conversion" error. lendude acknowledged this as a good reason to drop array_unique, and the MR was approved without it.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3608776: [regression] Route discovery isn't fully compatible with Symfony routes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +55/−5 · php +46/−5 · yml +9
  • Diff: d290c9a
  • Discussion: #3608776 · 4 contributors · 16 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

A regression introduced in #3311365 caused route discovery to reject routing.yml files that specified methods or schemes as a plain string (e.g., methods: 'GET') instead of a YAML array. Symfony's own Route constructor has always accepted either form, but Drupal's StaticRouteDiscoveryBase::createRoute() was narrowed to require arrays only. The fix restores compatibility: both methods and schemes now accept a string or an array, matching Symfony's behavior. The Gutenberg contrib module was the first reported breakage.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The regression originated in #3311365, which refactored route discovery to use PHP attributes. That change narrowed the $methods and $schemes parameters of StaticRouteDiscoveryBase::createRoute() from the implicit string|array that Symfony's Route constructor accepts to a strict array. Any routing.yml that used a bare string for either key (valid YAML and previously accepted by Symfony) would then fail at route compilation.

The fix widens those two parameter types back to string|array in the protected function createRoute() signature. The underlying Route constructor from Symfony already handles the string-to-array normalization, so no further change is needed in the method body. A new kernel test class YamlRouteDiscoveryTest and a companion fixture route router_test.string_method_scheme (with methods: 'GET' and schemes: 'https' as strings) cover the restored behavior.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 6, 2026
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (7 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • neclimdul (APQC)
  • joelpittet (The University of British Columbia)
  • godotislate
  • catch (Tag1 Consulting, Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3592946: ComponentNegotiator::negotiate() re-runs on every call when no replacement is found
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: 11.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +24/−3 · php +24/−3
  • Diff: 46e8817
  • Discussion: #3592946 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

On sites using Single Directory Components (SDC) with a subtheme, ComponentNegotiator::negotiate() was re-running its full component-definition scan on every call instead of returning a cached result. The issue reports roughly 8× slower negotiation for repeated calls within the same request. The fix is a one-line correction that makes the cache work properly for the common case where no component replacement exists.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

ComponentNegotiator::negotiate() stores its result in $this->cache keyed by a generated cache key. The previous cache-hit check used isset($this->cache[$cache_key] ?? NULL), which returns false when the cached value is NULL. Because doNegotiate() returns NULL when no replacement is found (the normal case for most components under a subtheme), the cache was effectively bypassed every time. doNegotiate() runs array_filter over the full set of component definitions on each miss, making the cost proportional to the total number of registered components multiplied by the number of find() calls per request.

The fix replaces the isset() pattern with array_key_exists($cache_key, $this->cache), which correctly treats a stored NULL as a valid cache hit. A new kernel test testNegotiateCachesNullResults in ComponentNegotiatorTest verifies the behavior by injecting a fake replacement definition after the first negotiate() call and confirming that the null result is still served from cache on the second call.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 31, 2026
  • Committed: July 13, 2026 (1 month and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • herved (European Commission and European Union Institutions, Agencies and Bodies)
  • amitgoyal (Acquia)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
